title: Derivative for ridge loss
body:
Collin suggests that Sarika try a completely different loss function to predict minutes listened using two features:
- xᵢ₁: minutes spent on assignments on day i
- xᵢ₂: temperature on day i

They define the following regularized loss function:
$$L(	heta_1,	heta_2)= rac{1}{2n}\sum_{i=1}**{n}(y_i-	heta_1x_{i1}-	heta_2x_{i2})**{2}+ rac{\lambda}{2}(	heta_1**{2}+	heta_2**{2})$$

Sarika decides to minimize this loss using gradient descent.

She starts from initial values:
$$	heta_1**{(0)}=0,\;	heta_2**{(0)}=0$$
She uses a learning rate $$\alpha=0.1$$ and a regularization term $$\lambda=0.5$$. Sarika uses new data to fit this model:
$$\mathbb{X}= egin{bmatrix}1&1\\2&0\\3&1\end{bmatrix}, \;\mathbb{Y}= egin{bmatrix}25\\10\\5\end{bmatrix}$$
